Title: Insight into VvGH3 genes evolutionary relationship from monocotyledons and dicotyledons reveals that VvGH3-9 negatively regulates the drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional validation of VvGH3-9 was performed via heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis, so drought-related phenotypes were measured in the Arabidopsis background while attributing gene function to the grape GH3 gene VvGH3-9.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. VvGH3-9 encodes a GH3 family protein with conserved GH3 domain(s) and an N-terminal leucine zipper motif and is localized to the nucleus and cytoplasm in plant cells.
2. In grape, VvGH3-9 shows tissue- and stage-specific expression, with higher transcript levels in vigorous tissues (e.g., seed fruit set, seed post fruit set, latent buds) and responsiveness to ABA, MeJA, IAA, and abiotic stresses including NaCl, PEG, and cold.
3. Overexpression of VvGH3-9 in Arabidopsis results in significantly increased VvGH3-9 transcript levels under 20% PEG and leads to higher IAA and ABA contents compared with WT after 72 h PEG treatment.
4. Under PEG-induced drought stress, VvGH3-9 overexpression lines exhibit higher H2O2 and MDA contents and relative electrolyte conductivity, lower activities of POD, SOD, and CAT and reduced proline accumulation, and more severe leaf wilting than WT.
5. The expression of six drought-responsive Arabidopsis genes (AtPOD, AtSOD, AtCAT, AtWRKY40, AtbZIP62, AtPYD1) is significantly lower in VvGH3-9 overexpression lines than in WT after 72 h of 20% PEG treatment.
6. Collectively, these data demonstrate that VvGH3-9 functions as a negative regulator of drought tolerance, decreasing drought resistance in the Arabidopsis model and indicating a similar negative role in grape drought resistance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data for VvGH3-9 (and other VvGH3-x genes) under NaCl, PEG, 4 °C, and ABA treatments, from NCBI GEO datasets GSE31662 and GSE31594.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VvGH3-9 acts as a negative regulator of drought tolerance: its overexpression in Arabidopsis reduces tolerance to PEG-induced drought stress by disrupting hormone balance and antioxidant defenses, leading to greater oxidative and membrane damage.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Overexpression of VvGH3-9 in Arabidopsis under 20% PEG increases endogenous IAA and ABA levels, elevates H2O2 and MDA contents and relative electrolyte conductivity, reduces proline accumulation and POD, SOD, and CAT activities, and is associated with reduced expression of drought-responsive genes such as AtPOD, AtSOD, AtCAT, AtWRKY40, AtbZIP62, and AtPYD1, thereby weakening antioxidant defense and enhancing membrane damage under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin (IAA) and ABA-mediated drought/osmotic stress response pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Overexpression of VvGH3-9 in Arabidopsis is associated with reduced expression of downstream drought-responsive genes AtPOD, AtSOD, AtCAT, AtWRKY40, AtbZIP62, and AtPYD1 under 20% PEG treatment for 72 h (VvGH3-9 overexpression --(repression/negative association)--> AtPOD/AtSOD/AtCAT/AtWRKY40/AtbZIP62/AtPYD1).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family protein containing conserved GH3 domain(s) and an N-terminal leucine zipper motif (motif 5).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm, based on 35S::VvGH3-9-GFP transient expression in Arabidopsis protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In grape, VvGH3-9 shows relatively high expression in vigorous tissues such as seed fruit set, seed post fruit set, and latent buds, and is induced in roots and leaves by ABA (strongly in roots, ~13-fold) and slightly by MeJA, while being down-regulated in leaves under NaCl, PEG, and 5 °C treatments for 24 h. Its transcript level decreases in berry skin as fruits ripen.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20% PEG 6000 treatment to impose drought/osmotic stress for up to 72 h in transgenic and WT Arabidopsis; additional assays of VvGH3-9 expression under NaCl (200 mmol L^-1), 10% PEG, 5 °C cold stress, and ABA, MeJA, and IAA treatments in grape tissues.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: GH3 gene family evolution and auxin-related drought stress response; functional characterization of VvGH3-9 in drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In three overexpression lines (OE-2, OE-8, OE-15), VvGH3-9 transcript levels increase about 6.7-, 6.9-, and 7.5-fold at 72 h of 20% PEG relative to 0 h. Antioxidant enzyme (POD, SOD, CAT) activities are significantly lower, while relative electrolyte conductivity, H2O2 and MDA contents, and membrane lipid peroxidation are significantly higher than WT at 48–72 h PEG treatment, and proline content is significantly lower in OE lines at 48–72 h.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under 20% PEG, leaves of VvGH3-9 overexpression Arabidopsis lines become more withered and show deeper leaf color than WT by 72 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: 'Pinot' grape plantlets in vitro and berry skins from field-grown 7-year-old vines; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia plants; Arabidopsis protoplasts from 4-week-old leaves; three VvGH3-9 overexpression Arabidopsis lines (OE-2, OE-8, OE-15).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of GH3 genes by BLAT and HMM domain search; cloning of VvGH3-9 CDS by PCR and homologous recombination into pART-CAM-FLAG and pBI221-GFP; Agrobacterium-mediated floral-dip transformation of Arabidopsis; PEG-mediated protoplast transfection; RT-qPCR for VvGH3-9 and stress-responsive gene expression; HPLC quantification of IAA and ABA; measurements of relative electrical conductivity, proline, H2O2, and MDA contents; enzymatic assays for POD, SOD, and CAT activities; DAB and NBT staining for ROS visualization; statistical analysis of expression data.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: VvGH3-9 was identified as part of the grape GH3 gene family using BLAT search and HMM domain PF03321.1 against the grape genome, and its CDS was amplified from 'Pinot Noir' (PN40024) by PCR and cloned into overexpression (pART-CAM-FLAG) and GFP (pBI221-GFP) vectors via homologous recombination.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because VvGH3-9 overexpression decreases drought tolerance, VvGH3-9 functions as a negative regulator of drought resistance in grape and represents a potential target for genetic improvement; reducing its expression or activity (e.g., by gene editing or marker-assisted selection) could enhance drought tolerance in grape cultivars.
